diagnosis: read-last-lines: The read_text_file description presented 'head' and 'tail' as equally prominent options without explicitly mapping 'tail' to 'last N lines' requests, causing the agent to occasionally explore with 'head' first. compare-two-configs: The read_multiple_files description used 'Always prefer this' which was too weak; it did not explicitly forbid read_text_file for exactly two files, so the agent occasionally fell back to sequential read_text_file calls. create-new-file: The write_file description forbade create_directory but did not explicitly state that parent directories are created automatically, nor did it forbid list_directory. The list_directory description never warned against using it as an existence check before write or move, making it look like a valid preliminary step. make-nested-directory: The create_directory description said 'creates nested directories in a single call' but was not emphatic enough that one call with the full final path is always sufficient; the agent repeatedly called it for the same path and parent paths. move-into-archive: The move_file description forbade create_directory and get_file_info but omitted list_directory, so the agent called list_directory to verify the destination. It also did not explicitly state that the destination must include the filename, causing uncertainty. find-by-extension: The search_files description was clear for tool choice; the one extra call was minor parameter exploration not driven by the description.

rationales.read_text_file: Explicitly maps 'tail' to last-lines requests and simplifies the multi-file instruction to prevent confusion.
rationales.read_multiple_files: Explicitly states the threshold is two files and forbids read_text_file for multiple files to prevent sequential calls.
rationales.write_file: States that parent directories are handled automatically and expands the prohibition to list_directory and get_file_info.
rationales.create_directory: Clarifies that one call with the full path is sufficient and prohibits repeated calls for the same path or its parents.
rationales.move_file: Explicitly forbids list_directory and clarifies that the destination must be the full path including the filename.
rationales.list_directory: Prevents the agent from using list_directory as an existence check before write or move operations.

overrides.read_text_file: Use this to read the contents of a single text file. Use the 'tail' parameter when the user asks for the last N lines, or the 'head' parameter for the first N lines. When reading two or more files, use read_multiple_files instead. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.read_multiple_files: Use this to read the contents of two or more text files in a single call, such as when comparing or analyzing them. Never use read_text_file for multiple files. Each file's content is returned with its path as a reference. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.write_file: Use this to create a new text file or overwrite an existing one with the exact content provided. Automatically creates missing parent directories and handles text encoding. Do not call list_directory, create_directory, or get_file_info first. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.create_directory: Use this only when the user explicitly asks to create a new directory or folder. Provide the full nested path in one call; all intermediate directories are created automatically. Never call this more than once for the same path, and never call it before write_file or move_file. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.move_file: Use this to move or rename a file or directory by providing the source and the full destination path including the filename. Do not call list_directory, create_directory, or get_file_info beforehand, even if the destination directory may not exist. The operation will fail if a file already exists at the destination. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.list_directory: Use this for a flat, non-recursive listing of a single directory. Results clearly distinguish files with [FILE] and directories with [DIR] prefixes. Do not use this to check whether a file or directory exists before calling read_text_file, write_file, or move_file. For recursive pattern searches, use search_files instead. Only works within allowed directories.
